2606.06458.title: In-Context Multiple Instance Learning
2606.06458.paper_summary: Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) addresses problems where supervision is available at the level of bags of instances and has been successfully applied in fields ranging from computational pathology to satellite imagery. Nevertheless, existing algorithms struggle in the low-label regime that characterizes many real-world applications. Flexible models overfit and rigid ones fail to adapt to the task at hand. We show that pretraining an in-context learner with a Perceiver-style architecture on synthetic data yields a model that can solve new tasks from a handful of labeled bags. At inference time, classification happens in a single forward pass and requires no gradient updates. We propose and investigate different synthetic data generators for bag-structured data and find that they capture complementary inductive biases. A model pretrained on a mixture of these generators inherits their per-task strengths and achieves the best average performance across twelve MIL benchmarks, outperforming supervised baselines that require task-specific training.
2606.06458.paper_summary_zh: 多實例學習（MIL）解決了在實例包層級上可用監督的問題，並已成功應用於從計算病理學到衛星影像等領域。<lb/>然而，現有算法在許多現實世界應用中所特徵的低標籤範疇中表現不佳。<lb/>靈活的模型過度擬合，而剛性模型則無法適應當前任務。<lb/>我們展示了在合成數據上使用Perceiver風格架構對上下文學習者進行預訓練，可以產生一個能夠從少量標記包中解決新任務的模型。<lb/>在推理時，分類發生在單次前向傳播中，並且不需要梯度更新。<lb/>我們提出並研究了不同的合成數據生成器，用於包結構數據，發現它們捕捉到互補的歸納偏差。<lb/>在這些生成器的混合上預訓練的模型繼承了它們的每個任務優勢，並在十二個MIL基準測試中達到最佳平均表現，超越了需要特定任務訓練的監督基線。
2606.06458.author: Alexander Möllers et.al.
2606.06458.authors: Alexander Möllers, Marvin Sextro, Julius Hense, Gabriel Dernbach, Klaus-Robert Müller
